summary: Parallel review: FAIL (8 critical, 2 warning, 4 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: Architectural assessment: clean module boundaries (synthesize owns the contract string + detector; flows owns labeling), single source of truth via exported constant, no string duplication, and the prior brittle strip-then-rewrap regex is gone. `hasSimplicityVeto` is a small, well-scoped defensive layer inside `computeVerdict`. No 🔴, no 🟡. Eval written to `task-5/eval-architect.md`.

🟡 .team/features/multi-perspective-code-review/tasks/task-5:1 — task-5 has no `handshake.json` and empty `artifacts/`; spec duplicates task-4 verbatim (commits 7097ba5 / 8d2aea3 are duplicates). Close as redundant or distinguish scope with a fresh handshake.

Spec is clear and testable; implementation at `flows.mjs:202` + `synthesize.mjs:55-78` matches; the three acceptance cases (any-role 🔴 FAIL / all-clean PASS / simplicity-only 🔴 FAIL with tag preserved) are green in the gate output. Scope is tight. Full eval at `.team/features/multi-perspective-code-review/tasks/task-5/eval-pm.md`.
- `computeVerdict` defensive case (🟡-tagged veto still FAILs) and 🔴-only-veto FAIL both locked.
- 🟡 simplicity correctly does NOT get the veto tag (build-verify-parallel-review.test.mjs:194).

🔵 test/synthesize.test.mjs:307 — No CLI-level test for `cmdSynthesize` with a 🔴 simplicity finding on the single (non-merge) flow; an stdin→JSON e2e assertion would protect the public output contract.
🔵 test/build-verify-parallel-review.test.mjs:89 — Add "veto tag survives sort with multiple 🔴 from different roles" to pin that the label isn't stripped during severity ordering.
No 🔴 or 🟡 findings. Eval written to `.team/features/multi-perspective-code-review/tasks/task-5/eval-tester.md`.

🔵 bin/lib/synthesize.mjs:58 — `f.text.includes(SIMPLICITY_VETO_TAG)` matches the tag anywhere on the line including in narrative prose; consider anchoring to the role-prefix position (e.g., `/^[🔴🟡🔵]\s*\[simplicity veto\]/u`). Optional.
No 🔴 or 🟡 simplicity findings. The four veto categories (dead code, premature abstraction, unnecessary indirection, gold-plating) all PASS:
